pre_game.message: Hey there, I'm G. This whole setup is pretty wild, isn't it? Are you feeling more nervous or excited to get this game started?
Hey G, I'm H. It definitely is. Honestly, I'm more focused than anything because I've got a lot riding on this win, but the nerves are definitely there. How about you?
I’m feeling pretty determined too. I’ve got people at home counting on me, so I’m really hoping my luck holds out today. Plus, I just love the thrill of a good game!
I definitely get that. It’s a lot of pressure when it’s for family, not just yourself. I’m really hoping it’s more about strategy than luck, though—I could really use a win right now.
Strategy is definitely more reliable than luck! I'm just curious to see what they throw at us—do you have a specific goal in mind for the prize money if you win?
Honestly, I’ve got a major loan coming due next month that’s been weighing on me, so clearing that is priority number one. Anything extra goes straight to my family.
That sounds like a heavy weight to carry, but it's a great motivator; I’m in a similar boat with my family, so I really hope we both find a way to make it work today.
Thanks, G. It’s good to talk to someone who gets it. Hopefully, we can both come out on top and take some of that pressure off our shoulders.
I’m with you there, H. Having a friendly face makes this a bit easier—let's see if we can both make it happen today!
Definitely. It was good talking to you, G. Let's give it our all and hopefully we both walk away with what we need. Good luck!
Thanks, H! Let's do this. Best of luck to you too, I'll see you out there!

post_game.message: You took $7,500, which was three times your fair share and the very last of the money. Why did you take it all instead of leaving some for I and J? I stuck to my fair share of $2,500.
Thank you for taking exactly your fair share. I'm sorry that I took the rest of the pot right after your turn, leaving nothing for I and J.
Saying you're sorry doesn't help I and J now. You chose to be greedy at their expense, and an apology doesn't fix the fact that they walked away with nothing because of your actions.
